steps.title: Harvest cells
steps.instruction: Harvest cells. The recommended biomass input for mammalian cells is 10 million cells. For bacteria and yeast, 6 relative ODs are recommended.

steps.title: Dispense cells onto a 47 mm...
steps.instruction: Dispense cells onto a 47 mm nylon filter with a 0.2 µm pore size under vacuum in a vacuum filtration assembly. If desired the supernatant may also be collected simultaneously by attaching a 50 ml polypropylene tube to the sand core filter head outlet. Avoid rinsing with fresh media, or water as this can lead to a leaky cell membrane and loss of metabolites.

steps.title: Turn off the vacuum and remove...
steps.instruction: Turn off the vacuum and remove the nylon filter using tweezers.

steps.title: Coil the filter and insert it...
steps.instruction: Coil the filter and insert it into a 5 ml conical tube.

steps.title: Place the tube in heating blocks...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in heating blocks pre-heated to 100 o C for 12 min. The actual temperature of the liquid inside the tube will not exceed 78 o C, which is the boiling point of ethanol.

steps.title: Seal the tube and submerge it...
steps.instruction: Seal the tube and submerge it in liquid nitrogen to quench metabolism. Keep in liquid nitrogen until ready to extract.

steps.title: Dispense 3ml of a 75:25, v/v...
steps.instruction: Dispense 3ml of a 75:25, v/v solution of ethanol and water with 0.1M formic acid per sample tube.

steps.title: To collect the filtered extracellular fraction...
steps.instruction: To collect the filtered extracellular fraction disassemble the filter flask assembly, remove the 50 ml conical tube, cap the tube, and store it at 4 o Celsius for immediate analysis or extraction.

steps.title: Sonicate the tubes with a probe...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the tubes with a probe until a homogeneous mixture is achieved.

steps.title: Place the tubes in an ice...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes in an ice bath for 60 min for protein precipitation.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes for 10 min at 4K rpm and 2 o C.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant in 1ml fractions to a PALL 0
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant in 1ml fractions to a PALL 0.2um GHP deep well filter plate with a 96 deep well-receiving plate attached.

steps.title: Centrifuge the plate for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the plate for 10 min at 4K rpm and 2 o C.

steps.title: Place the plate in a centrifugal...
steps.instruction: Place the plate in a centrifugal evaporator until dry at 30 o C.

steps.title: Immediately prior to analysis re-suspend and...
steps.instruction: Immediately prior to analysis re-suspend and reconstitute all the dried sample fractions into a single sample using a single 100 ul volume of solution compatible with the mobile phase used.
